prompt:
Use X + SuperGrok. Query exactly: "how are people actually setting up autonomous agents? find me the tweets of people actually doing this"

Return strict JSON with this schema:
{
  "query": string,
  "oneSentenceTake": string,
  "tweets": [{"author": string, "handle": string, "text": string, "url": string, "whyItMatters": string}],
  "people": [{"name": string, "handle": string, "url": string, "evidence": string}],
  "setupPatterns": [string]
}
Rules: include at least 15 tweets; prioritize people showing real builds, repos, demos, or deployment details.

status.output:
{
  "query": "how are people actually setting up autonomous agents? find me the tweets of people actually doing this",
  "oneSentenceTake": "Developers are shifting from experimental chatbots to production-grade autonomous systems by leveraging modular frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and MCP servers combined with persistent memory and local/cloud orchestration.",
  "tweets": [
    {"author": "Rhys Sullivan", "handle": "@RhysSullivan", "text": "Ok I\'ve built this: 1. You write your tools in an MCP server 2. Deploy it to your own infra or we can host it 3. Each gets its own branch. Supports Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Pi.", "url": "https://x.com/chulcher", "whyItMatters": "Enables headless orchestration of coding agents with lifecycle management and multi-model support."},
